title: Data Type Mapping Between Uniface and Java
breadcrumbs: Data Type Mapping Between Uniface and Java
text: Data Type Mapping Between Uniface and Java Data type conversions are all handled by Uniface and the Uniface*.class files included in the ujci30.jar file. Table 1. Default Data Type Mappings Between Uniface and Java ProcScript data type Default Java data type Remarks String String Java uses unicode Numeric int Limited to Java capacity Float double Limited to Java capacity Boolean boolean Date UnifaceDateTime A Uniface class Time UnifaceDateTime A Uniface class Datetime UnifaceDateTime A Uniface class Entity UnifaceEntity A Uniface class Raw array of byte Occurrence UnifaceOccurrence A Uniface class Image java.awt.Image The following Uniface data types are not currently supported for Java: Linear Date Linear Time Linear Datetime The following table lists Uniface to Java data type mappings that differ from the default types, when Java-specific signature information is available: Table 2. Other Data Type Mappings Between Uniface and Java ProcScript data type Java data types String char , Character , String (Default) Numeric byte , Byte , short , Short , int (Default), Integer , long , Long , String Float float , Float , double (Default), Double , String Boolean boolean (Default), Boolean Image Data Type The Image data type allows image conversion to Java image types from more types than are supported by version 1.3 of the JDK, which only supports .gif and .jpeg image types. Uniface only supports conversion from Uniface to Java. Conversions from Java to Uniface can only be done as binary data, .gif data, for example. If the image is a .gif or .jpeg , it is more efficient to use the createImage() or getImage() methods from the Java Abstract Windows Toolkit (AWT) to convert to a Java image, because the extra Uniface to Java image format conversions are bypassed. If an image cannot be converted, due to an unrecognized format or null data, then a null pointer is returned. No exception is thrown. Note: One image can contain more than one picture. Uniface can convert only single pictures. This also applies to icons which have extra background pictures. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
